name: Kettlebell Single-Leg Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Loading one leg with a kettlebell greatly increases per-leg tension; hardest in the deep stretch.
cues: Use light fingertip support only - let the calf do the work
Full heel drop, then rise all the way onto the toes
execution_steps: Stand with the ball of one foot on a block or step, heel hanging off, holding a kettlebell in the same-side hand.
Rest your free hand on a wall or rack for balance.
Lift the non-working foot so all weight is on the working leg.
Lower the working heel into a full stretch below the block.
Drive up onto the toes to full plantarflexion, pause, then lower under control.
good_when: You want to overload each calf without heavy machines
You're correcting a side-to-side calf imbalance
bad_when: Poor balance limits how hard you can load the calf
Grip fails before the calf does with a heavy bell
common_errors: Pushing through the balance hand to assist the raise
Cutting the stretch short at the bottom
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
